headlines.title: Water Is Quietly Becoming One of the Biggest Risks in Energy
headlines.summary: A decade ago, China was widely expected to become a shale powerhouse. By some estimates, it holds more technically recoverable shale gas than the United States, and there was real optimism that it could replicate the U.S. shale revolution. So far, that surge hasn’t materialized.

headlines.title: Canada Scores Record Trade Surplus on High Oil
headlines.summary: Canada has recorded its first trade surplus in six months, defying forecasts of a deficit thanks to surging oil and gold prices. Canada’s merchandise trade balance swung to a $1.78 billion surplus in March against expectations of a shortfall of $2.88 billion, with total exports r

headlines.title: U.S. Energy Secretary: Iran Cuts Oil Output as Exports Collapse
headlines.summary: Iran has cut oil production by roughly 400,000 barrels per day (bpd) as exports stall and storage runs out, according to U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright. “It looks like they’ve likely already cut back their production, maybe by 400,000 barrels a day,” Wright said in an intervi

headlines.title: Canadian Natural Says Pipeline Needed to Unlock Oil Sands Growth
headlines.summary: Canada’s oil sands can grow, but getting the barrels out of the country is a problem. This is a constraint that has defined the sector for years. headlines.title: U.S. Cracks Down on Iraq Oil Links to Iran
headlines.summary: The United States has sanctioned Iraq’s deputy oil minister and several Iran-aligned militia leaders, stepping directly into a part of the oil trade that has been operating in the gray since the war began. The Treasury Department accused Deputy Oil Minister Ali Maarij Al-Bahadly

headlines.title: Oil Supply Shock Worsens amid Plunging Petroleum Inventories
headlines.summary: Global crude oil and fuel inventories are crashing at a record speed as the supply shock from the Middle East is too big to absorb without stock depletion. While the futures markets trade on sentiment and hopes that a U.S.-Iran deal could soon lead to the reopening of the Strait

headlines.title: EU: Airlines Should Pay Passengers for Cancelations due to Fuel Price Surge
headlines.summary: Airlines will have to continue reimbursing travelers if they cancel flights due to high jet fuel prices, EU Sustainable Transport and Tourism Commissioner, Apostolos Tzitzikostas, told the Financial Times in an interview published on Thursday. headlines.title: Iran’s Oil Industry Under Pressure as U.S. Naval Blockade Begins to Bite
headlines.summary: An Iranian energy official just conceded something in a surprise admission that the US naval blockade has begun to bite the Islamic Republic's oil industry. headlines.title: Angola's First New Refinery in 50 Years Ships Its First Fuel Cargoes
headlines.summary: The Cabinda oil refinery in Angola, the first that was built in the country since it gained independence from Portugal 50 years ago, has started shipping fuels to the domestic and international markets, in a relief to the fuel supply stress due to the Iran war.
